phrasal_verbs.meaning: stop doing sth you do regularly
make a player leave a game (eg, football)

A.title: Original course practice
A.instructions: Complete each original item using the target language from this lesson.

across.clue: If he wins this match, he'll be the world ......! (8)
I'm thinking of joining a ...... to get more exercise. (3)
Our basketball ...... said that I can play on Saturday! (5)
The ...... blew his whistle and the game started. (7)
Mark's band play traditional ...... music – they often perform at country fairs and festivals. (4)

down.clue: I'm sorry, but you have to be a ...... of the golf club to play here. (6)
My ...... was a brilliant player and I didn't manage to win the match. (8)
Tom is really good at cards. He would never ......! (5)
Lisa's ...... has just reached number one with their new song! (5)
I took a big ...... by doing the parachute jump, but I loved every second of it! (4)

B.title: Original course practice
B.instructions: Complete each original item using the target language from this lesson.
B.passage:
Original passage for Fun and games

Leo joined a study group because the group wanted fresh practice for course practice. The tutor wrote (1) ...... (2) ...... (3) ...... (4) ...... on the board and asked everyone to explain their choices. Later, the group compared (5) ...... (6) ...... (7) ...... with the rule from the lesson. The text is new, but it practises the same course practice.
